run_summary: Portfolio is flat YTD with two positions opened today (MASI and AYI). MASI thesis remains intact — the 8-K signaled a closed divestiture with stock rationally flat on 3x volume. AYI has deteriorated into pure sympathy selling (-10.37% over 5 days, -6.5% today) on CPI/Iran headlines with no company catalyst — thesis weakening. New flag pool includes three defense names caught in geopolitical momentum (ALK underdone on fuel relief, KTOS and MRCY overdone on de-escalation fades), plus a CRSP re-entry that fails the confidence bar. Decisions: hold both open positions, buy ALK as exploratory on fuel-cost asymmetry, watch KTOS/MRCY/CRSP for confirmation, skip others.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Trump's claim of Iran deal 'subject to finalization' → oil lower → airline fuel relief
entries.thesis: Iran deal framing → oil down ~2% → airline fuel cost relief, layered on risk-on tape and IWM strength. ALK at ~$5B cap is a smaller airline with high fuel sensitivity. If the Iran deal actually finalizes in 'next few days' as Trump claims, oil leg-down extends and ALK's fuel cost tailwind compounds. The move is large but airline operating leverage to fuel is enormous — could underextend if crude breaks lower.
entries.what_kills: Iran deal fails to materialize and oil reverses; or an ALK-specific 8-K surfaces showing a labor cost or operational issue that re-frames this as a relief bounce off a prior decline.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The thesis assumes the Iran deal will 'finalize in next few days' and drive crude lower, but the move has already captured 9.3% of the expected relief in just five days, leaving minimal alpha if the catalyst executes as priced. Bear case: The five-day move of +9.3% already reflects substantial anticipation of fuel-cost relief from the oil decline. Haiku acknowledges the move is 'already extended' but frames this as 'buying early legs of a potential secondary fuel-cost leg'—a backwards read of the entry signal. If the Iran deal materializes as flagged, crude may have already moved enough to price most of the airline benefit into ALK's current level. Conversely, if the deal stalls or reverses (flagged as a kill condition), ALK has no other fundamental support and reverts to the 'prior decline' that preceded this bounce. The exploratory 6% position size is appropriate for idea strength but conflicts with a setup where price has already run hard on the same catalyst framework. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Alaska Air is flagged UNDERDONE conf 3 on a clean, asymmetric fuel-cost setup: Iran deal framing → oil down ~2% → airline fuel relief, layered on risk-on tape and IWM strength. Move is large (11.95% today, +9.3% over 5 days) but airline operating leverage to crude is enormous — a 2% oil decline translates to 8-12% fuel-cost relief for a small carrier. The flag explicitly names the mechanism (fuel sensitivity), the catalyst URL (Trump Iran deal claim), and the confirmation test (crude continues lower, other small airlines follow). Five-day move is already substantial (+9.3%), but if crude breaks further on actual deal finalization in 'next few days,' the thesis compounds. This is a 6% exploratory position because the setup is real and the catalyst URL is clean, but the move is already extended and the Iran deal may not materialize. Entry here buys the early legs of a potential secondary fuel-cost leg if crude breaks lower. Positioned 6% per exploratory tier.)

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes the Iran deal will 'finalize in next few days' and drive crude lower, but the move has already captured 9.3% of the expected relief in just five days, leaving minimal alpha if the catalyst executes as priced.
red_team.critique: The five-day move of +9.3% already reflects substantial anticipation of fuel-cost relief from the oil decline. Haiku acknowledges the move is 'already extended' but frames this as 'buying early legs of a potential secondary fuel-cost leg'—a backwards read of the entry signal. If the Iran deal materializes as flagged, crude may have already moved enough to price most of the airline benefit into ALK's current level. Conversely, if the deal stalls or reverses (flagged as a kill condition), ALK has no other fundamental support and reverts to the 'prior decline' that preceded this bounce. The exploratory 6% position size is appropriate for idea strength but conflicts with a setup where price has already run hard on the same catalyst framework.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy on Iran headlines + IWM strength
entries.thesis: KTOS up 7% but five_day_change is -7.3% — this looks like a bounce off a defense pullback rather than fresh strength. The puzzling part: if Iran is settling, the geopolitical premium that drove defense names higher should unwind, not extend. ITA +5% suggests the market is reading 'Trump shows force → deals get done → more defense spend, not less,' but that read may not hold once the deal language firms up. KTOS is high-beta defense; the move likely overshoots and fades when the de-escalation reality sinks in.
entries.what_kills: Deal falls through and conflict escalates, in which case KTOS extends. Or DoD budget news / specific Kratos contract award surfaces that justifies the move on fundamentals not geopolitics.
entries.reasoning: Kratos is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 on the basis of a logical paradox: up 7.22% today but down 7.29% over 5 days, suggesting today's move is a bounce off a defense pullback, not fresh strength. The thesis argues that if Iran is settling, the geopolitical premium should unwind, not extend — and KTOS's 7% pop is likely a misreading of Trump's 'force = deals' that will fade once deal language firms up. This is a coherent fade thesis. However, the flag lacks a catalyst URL, and the 'timing' of the fade is vague ('within a week'). The move is not yet extreme enough to short with confidence (up only 7% in a single day is not obviously overdone for a defense name on risk-on tape). Wait for ITA to roll over and/or for KTOS to make a higher high before shorting — a single-day bounce is not a profitable short signal on its own.

entries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
entries.catalyst: Defense sympathy + risk-on tape; no company-specific catalyst
entries.thesis: MRCY up nearly 12% on 1.25x volume with no SEC filing and no earnings catalyst — yet 5-day change is barely positive. That pattern says the move is concentrated entirely in today's session and likely driven by a short squeeze or sympathy with ITA strength, not fundamentals. Defense small-caps with high short interest often spike sharply on risk-on days then fade. Without a confirming catalyst this looks like a momentum/squeeze move that overshoots.
entries.what_kills: A Mercury-specific contract win or analyst upgrade surfaces; or short interest data shows the move was structurally supported by genuine new buyers, not squeeze mechanics.
entries.reasoning: Mercury Systems is flagged OVERDONE conf 3 on a squeeze/momentum pattern: up 11.71% with 1.25x volume but only +1.27% over 5 days, suggesting concentration in today's session. No 8-K, no earnings, no catalyst URL. The thesis rests on the idea that defense small-caps with high short interest spike on risk-on days then fade. This is a plausible pattern (the 5-day flatness does suggest the move is single-day momentum), but without short-interest data or a catalyst URL, the evidence is circumstantial. The move is more extended than KTOS (+11.71% vs +7.22%), which makes it a more credible fade candidate — but the lack of hard data on short positions and the absence of a catalyst URL place this in WATCH territory. If ITA rolls over and MRCY doesn't fade symmetrically, the hypothesis is falsified; if it does fade, re-flag as OVERDONE conf 4.

entries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-04 with items 5.02, 5.03, 5.07, 9.01 — pattern consistent with annual meeting results
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 dated 2026-06-04 fit the pattern of an annual meeting filing (5.07 = vote results, 5.03 = charter amendments approved, 5.02 = board reshuffle/director election). That's normally NOT a material negative — it's housekeeping. But on a risk-off day with XBI -3.6%, the market is selling first and reading later. If the 5.02 turns out to be a routine board election rather than a CEO/CSO departure, the magnitude (~3x XBI move) is excessive for a clinical-stage name with no pipeline news.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unplanned CFO/CMO/CEO departure, or 5.03 includes a dilutive authorized-share increase that signals impending raise — either would justify the move and then some
entries.reasoning: CRSP carries a re-entry warning: closed 3 days ago for a +0.28% gain after flagging the same name as UNDERDONE (sector rally + routine governance 8-K). New flag re-classifies CRSP as OVERDONE on the basis of a -8.97% single-day move during XBI risk-off (-3.6%), arguing 3x sector beta is excessive for a clinical-stage name with routine annual-meeting filing (5.02 director election, 5.03 charter amendments, 5.07 vote results). Re-entry discipline requires confidence >= 4 and genuinely new information. This flag is confidence 3, so it fails the first bar automatically. The execution layer will downgrade a BUY to WATCH regardless of intent. Moreover, the 'new information' argument does not hold: the flag essentially re-argues the same thesis as the prior UNDERDONE call ('the move is excessive because the 8-K is routine'), just in the opposite direction (down instead of up). No update to the 8-K contents, no new pipeline news, no analyst change. The prior exit reasoning cited exactly this thesis conflict and recommended waiting for catalog clarity. Hold WATCH; if CRSP bounces hard tomorrow on confirmation that the 8-K is routine (press release, analyst note), re-enter at better levels with conf 4+ conviction.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector-wide semicap rally; no company-specific catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with SMH +6.75% on a risk-on, semis-leadership day. ONTO is a high-beta semicap that routinely amplifies SMH moves ~2x. Volume only at average (1.03x) argues against a name-specific catalyst — this looks like beta + short cover in a thin float on a melt-up day, not idiosyncratic news.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K or news item surfaces showing a contract win, guidance raise, or analyst upgrade specific to ONTO that would re-cast this as underdone.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Risk-on tape + housing/builder bid; older 5.02 filing context
entries.thesis: BLDR has a recent 5.02 (officer/director departure or appointment) filing from 2026-06-05 — six days before today's move. The move is occurring on below-average volume (0.69x) into a strong risk-on tape with rate-sensitive names bid. The 5.02 by itself doesn't tell us whether the change was bullish (new CEO, market liked it) or bearish (CFO departure, would weigh on stock). Hard to commit a direction without reading the filing text.
entries.what_kills: Today's move is purely beta to IWM/housing tape and the 5.02 is irrelevant noise.

entries.name: Masimo Corporation
entries.catalyst: Multi-item 8-K consistent with closing of a previously-telegraphed divestiture or spin-off
entries.thesis: The 8-K bundles Item 1.02 (terminated agreement), 2.01 (completed disposition), 3.01 (delisting/listing rule), 3.03 (security holder rights modification), 5.01 (change in control), 5.02 (officer/director change), and 5.03 (charter amendment). That combination is the fingerprint of a closed spin-off or divestiture of the consumer/Sound United business — long anticipated by activists. Stock flat on triple volume suggests the market views the transaction terms as in-line with what was already priced in. Magnitude (zero move) is rational given the event was telegraphed.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K reveals an unexpected dilutive financing, a surprise CEO/leadership shake-up, or terms materially worse than expected, the flat tape becomes the mispricing and downside opens up.

entries.name: Insmed Incorporated
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filing or news in our feed
entries.thesis: No filings, no news, modest 1.3x volume on a -7% day with XBI -1.9%. Move is roughly 4x the sector beta but I can't articulate a specific bull or bear thesis without knowing the cause. Could be brensocatib commercial concern, a clinical leak, or a single fund unwind.
entries.what_kills: If a clean explanation appears (broker conference comment, secondary offering rumor), the call should be re-evaluated immediately.

entries.name: WEX Inc.
entries.thesis: WEX -7.4% on essentially average volume (1.02x), no 8-K, no news. WEX has dual exposure to fleet fuel cards (oil sensitivity, but generally positive when prices rise via spread) and corporate travel payments. Direction of oil sensitivity is ambiguous — historically WEX has been a beneficiary of higher fuel prices, not a victim. Can't construct a clean conf-3 thesis either direction.
entries.what_kills: Tape stabilizes and stock recovers — suggests illiquid mid-cap algo overshoot.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
entries.thesis: VECO ripping ~10% on ~3x volume while SMH is down 1.2% is highly unusual and suggests a company-specific catalyst (analyst upgrade, contract win, M&A chatter, or industry conference comments) not visible in our 8-K/news feed. Without identifiable catalyst, direction of fade vs. follow-through cannot be called with conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If the move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental driver, fade likely; if a major contract is confirmed (e.g., advanced packaging for HBM), continuation likely

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apparent rotation into rate-sensitive / housing complex ahead of CPI
entries.thesis: POOL up ~6% on 1.7x volume with no 8-K and no news. Likely tied to the rotation into housing/rate-sensitive names (XLRE +2.13%) on softer rate expectations into tomorrow's CPI. BLDR also up 6% supports housing-rotation thesis. This is more of a thematic move than a stock-specific mispricing — UNCLEAR because magnitude (6%+) seems large for pure rotation absent a specific catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Hot CPI print tomorrow would unwind the rate-sensitive rotation and these names likely give back gains

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.thesis: AVAV down 4.4% today and 13.6% over 5 days while ITA is up 1.4% and other defense names like DCO are up 4%. This is significant divergence from a strong sector. No 8-K, no news in feed — suggests possible secondary offering, contract loss, broker downgrade, or earnings positioning. Without catalyst visibility, cannot assess if oversold (UNDERDONE bounce) or just-starting (continuation down).
entries.what_kills: If AVAV reports earnings soon and is being de-risked into print, no mispricing — just positioning

entries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
entries.catalyst: Semi sector sympathy/catch-up trade; no company-specific signal
entries.thesis: LSCC was down 5-day before today and is closing the gap with the SMH rally. Move is consistent with short-covering on a low-volatility risk-on day rather than a fundamental catalyst — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled. The fact it underperformed the 5-day window while peers rallied suggests today is mean-reversion within the sector.
entries.what_kills: If it fades intraday tomorrow back to flat vs SMH — confirms one-day technical bounce

entries.name: Entegris, Inc.
entries.catalyst: AI/semi sector unwind; no company-specific catalyst found
entries.thesis: ENTG is high-beta to wafer-fab capex; with SMH down ~9% and ORCL/AVGO/NVDA all down hard on AI spend rerating, a double-digit drop in ENTG is in line with peers (VECO, ONTO, AEIS all down 7-8%). No company-specific 8-K, no earnings within window — this is sector beta, magnitude appropriate.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K appearing late showing a guide cut or customer-specific issue — would reframe this as company-specific and possibly UNDERDONE to the downside

entries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Defensive rotation; no company-specific signal in catalyst feeds
entries.thesis: TXRH up on a day when XLY is down ~2% is the rotation tell — investors moving into defensive consumer names with steady traffic. No company-specific catalyst visible, but the relative move (TXRH vs. XLY ~8 points of dispersion) is consistent with classic risk-off rotation into staples-adjacent restaurants. Magnitude is appropriate to the regime shift; not a mispricing.
entries.what_kills: An undisclosed analyst upgrade or M&A rumor surfacing — would reframe as idiosyncratic and possibly UNDERDONE
